dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9164 IH: Funding Refrigeration Equipment for Student Health Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Thompson of Pennsylvania (for himself and Mr. Courtney) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to require the Secretary of Agriculture to make grants to eligible entities to acquire and install milk storage-related equipment for use in elementary schools and secondary schools, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Funding Refrigeration Equipment for Student Health Act of 2026 or the FRESH Act.

subsection.text: The Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1751 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:

The Secretary shall carry out a program to make grants, on a competitive basis, to eligible entities to acquire and install cold storage equipment for storing milk or milk dispensers for use in elementary schools and secondary schools.

To be eligible to receive a grant under this section, an eligible entity shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.
The Federal share of a project carried out using a grant awarded under this section shall not be greater than 75 percent.
There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $4,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.

subparagraph.text: a local educational agency or a school food authority administering or operating a school meal program;
a consortium that includes a local educational agency or school food authority described in subparagraph (A), a tribal organization, or both.

paragraph.text: The terms elementary school, local educational agency, and secondary school have the meaning given those terms in section 8101 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 7801).
The term milk dispenser means a machine that stores and chills milk in bulk at a consistent temperature and allows a user to dispense the milk directly into a cup or other container.
The term school food authority has the meaning given the term in section 210.2 of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation).
The term tribal organization has the meaning given the term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (25 U.S.C. 5304).

subsection.text: Section 3 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1752) is amended by striking sections 13 and 17 and inserting sections 13, 17, and 30.
